method_atom: A macroscopic continuum field theory derived from first-principles conservation/field equations, in which the effective transport coefficients (conductivity, permittivity) are frequency-dependent and the medium's linear response is analyzed to identify which physical process dominates. When a diffusive transport mechanism dominates over direct field propagation, the resulting fluctuation power spectrum acquires a power-law inverse-frequency (1/f) scaling, providing a mechanism that reconciles apparently contradictory response measurements.
object_atom: The extracellular electric potentials (local field potentials) generated by neural activity in cortical/brain tissue, and the measured frequency dependence of electric conduction in that biological medium.
brief_reason: The first-principles derivation of a frequency-dependent macroscopic response and the diffusion-dominated generation of a 1/f fluctuation spectrum is the transferable machinery, while local field potentials in cortical tissue is the neuroscience-specific object; the two are cleanly separable.
